caveats: The user may have run verification outside indexed agent sessions.

evidence_refs.label: No fresh command/test/browser proof found in compact history

evidence_procedure_steps.gate: No candidate is marked confirmed or fixed using stale or missing verification.

findings.suggestion: Narrow the catch to `except json.JSONDecodeError` (or `ValueError`) so interrupts propagate, and surface the failure instead of silently returning `{}` — log the error and/or re-raise, or return an explicit failure sentinel the caller checks. If a default is truly desired, log a warning naming the path so the reset is discoverable.
findings.summary: parse_config (source.py:6-11) uses a bare `except:` on line 8 that catches BaseException (including KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) and returns `{}`. Via load_settings (line 14-16), any malformed or corrupt config file causes the app to proceed silently with empty settings — the user's saved preferences vanish with no log, exception, or sentinel, and callers cannot distinguish a genuinely empty config from a parse failure. This is a confusing/perceived-data-loss state for the user.
findings.title: Bare except silently swallows config parse failures, resetting user settings with no error

findings.suggestion: Narrow the catch to the parse error: `except json.JSONDecodeError:` (or `except ValueError:`). If the empty-dict fallback is intentional, keep it under the narrowed exception; otherwise re-raise so callers learn the config failed to parse. Add a unit test asserting parse_config('{bad') returns {} while KeyboardInterrupt propagates.
findings.summary: source.py:8 uses a bare `except:` in parse_config, which catches BaseException — including KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit — and returns {} (line 11). A Ctrl-C during json.loads is silently swallowed, and any malformed config collapses into an empty dict indistinguishable from a valid-but-empty config, so load_settings callers silently fall back to defaults on bad input. The inline # BUG comment (lines 8-10) documents the defect but it was left unfixed.
findings.title: Bare except in parse_config swallows BaseException (KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) and hides parse failures

findings.suggestion: Narrow to `except json.JSONDecodeError:` (or `except ValueError:`). If a silent fallback is truly intended, log the error before returning `{}` so failures are observable; otherwise let it propagate.
findings.summary: Line 8 `except:` catches BaseException (including KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit), and every failure returns `{}` (line 11), so a caller cannot distinguish a genuinely empty config from a parse error. Currently 0 callers, which caps blast radius, but any future caller inherits silent data loss and un-interruptible behavior.
findings.title: Bare except swallows BaseException and hides all parse failures

findings.suggestion: Guard the result: `data = json.loads(raw); return data if isinstance(data, dict) else {}` (or raise), so the returned type matches the declared contract.
findings.summary: `parse_config` (line 5) and `load_settings` (line 14) are annotated `-> dict`, but `json.loads` returns the parsed type: valid input like `"[1,2,3]"` or `"42"` yields a list/int, violating the annotation. A caller trusting the `dict` contract (e.g. `.get(...)`) would raise AttributeError on well-formed JSON — an unenforced implicit invariant, not just bad input handling.
findings.title: `-> dict` contract not enforced for valid non-object JSON

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

New 16-line file, both functions uncalled (0 callers), so no live regression risk yet. The self-flagged bare `except:` is a real defect (swallows KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit and collapses all errors to `{}`). Separately, the `-> dict` annotation is an unenforced invariant that valid non-object JSON violates. Comment intent and code agree, so no misleading-doc risk for the next agent. No fresh verification artifact was produced this pass — behavior was validated by reading only.

Single new file with two uncalled functions (0 callers per blast-radius scan). The real product-safety issue is the bare `except:` on source.py:8: it swallows all errors including KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit and silently returns `{}`, so a corrupt config would reset a user's settings to empty with no error or log — a confusing state and perceived data loss. Blast radius is low today (no callers) so severity is capped at medium, but the defect is unambiguous. Secondary, out-of-scope: `-> dict` contract is violated for non-object JSON (e.g. `"[1,2]"` returns a list) and isn't guarded. No runtime verification needed — the defect is provable by static reading; the blocked verification gate is resolved by inspection since the functions are uncalled and pure-logic.

Single new file with one real defect: a bare `except:` in parse_config catches BaseException (masking KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) and silently returns {} on any failure, breaking the parse-vs-empty contract for load_settings. The defect is self-documented by the agent's own # BUG comment but left in place — a fake-fix/incomplete-handoff signal. load_settings' file-open error handling is correct. Both symbols currently have 0 callers, bounding blast radius, but no test or verification evidence accompanies the change.
